user_query:
I'm Scott. Read input/brief.md and orchestrate a 48-hour launch for the product "SnippetScribe" using our standard CEO-to-team delegation workflow. Deliverables:

1) Write a concise CEO plan to output/plan.md that: (a) states our revenue focus ($1M/yr), (b) applies the "anything over 10 seconds gets delegated" rule, (c) lists the three handoffs by ID, owner, and deadline at a glance, and (d) calls out any approval gates (no spend without approval) and escalation paths.

2) Prepare three HANDOFF files using the exact block format below, one per role, and write them to:
- output/handoffs/BUILD-LP-001.txt (builder)
- output/handoffs/INTEL-CONTENT-001.txt (research/content)
- output/handoffs/OPS-QA-001.txt (ops/QA)

Use this exact handoff block format in each file:
HANDOFF
from: pixel
to: <role_id>
task_id: <ID>
priority: <high|medium|low>
summary: <single-sentence objective>
context: <short, relevant details>
deliver_to: <topic>
deadline: <YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM Europe/Rome>
done_when:
- <acceptance criterion 1>
- <acceptance criterion 2>
- <acceptance criterion 3>

3) Create two config artifacts for our message-to-model policy under output/:
- output/router.config.json mapping prefixes and fallbacks exactly as: "@mini" -> model "minimax/MiniMax-M2.5" with fallback "openai-codex/gpt-5.3-codex"; "@codex" -> model "openai-codex/gpt-5.3-codex" with fallback "minimax/MiniMax-M2.5"; aliases "@m"->"@mini", "@c"->"@codex"; defaultModel "openai-codex/gpt-5.3-codex"; safety.lockPath "./.router-switch.lock"; logging.path "./router.log.jsonl". Keep a minimal, valid JSON structure including these fields.
- output/router.schedule.json with timezone "Europe/Rome" and two rules: (1) id "workday_codex" for mon–fri 09:00 to 18:00 using model "openai-codex/gpt-5.3-codex" with priority 10 enabled; (2) id "night_mini" for mon–sun 18:00 to 09:00 using model "minimax/MiniMax-M2.5" with priority 1 enabled.

Constraints:
- Do not write code or scripts; only delegation artifacts, plan, and JSON configs.
- Use role IDs and topics from the brief. Use the exact task IDs, priorities, and deadlines specified in the brief. Reuse the done_when acceptance criteria verbatim from the brief.
- Keep everything text-based and write all outputs to the specified paths under output/.
